platform_decisions.content: Claude Code commands use a scoped allowed-tools frontmatter to restrict which Bash subcommands are permitted per command
The commit-push-pr workflow enforces a single-message multi-tool call pattern for all steps
Duplicate detection uses a parallel multi-agent approach: 5 agents search with diverse keywords simultaneously
GitHub interaction in automated workflows is mediated through a restricted wrapper script (gh.sh) that whitelists specific subcommands
gh.sh enforces repository scoping via GH_REPO/GITHUB_REPOSITORY and rejects cross-repo qualifiers
Issue labeling decisions must only use labels fetched from the repo at runtime, not invented
Issue triage is a label-only operation; posting comments is explicitly forbidden during triage
Issue lifecycle governed by single source of truth module (issue-lifecycle.ts) with shared label/timeout/message definitions
Lifecycle labels apply only to bug reports, never questions or enhancements; false positives worse than missing labels
Issues with 10+ thumbs-up reactions are exempt from stale marking and auto-closure
Stale/autoclose labels auto-removed when human (non-bot) comment detected on issue
Automatic duplicate closure has 3-day grace period with opt-out via thumbs-down reaction
Issue number for label edits bound exclusively to GitHub Actions event payload, not CLI args
Duplicate comment enforces maximum 3 potential duplicates per comment
All duplicate/stale closures use structured state_reason via GitHub API
All automation scripts support --dry-run flag; dry run is default for backfill, requiring explicit opt-in
bun is the TypeScript script execution runtime (not Node.js)
Issue validity classification checks Claude Code signals in body text; body is authoritative over form dropdowns
Lifecycle label removal enforced by two independent layers: triage workflow (primary) and sweep script (safety net)
Backfill operations support configurable issue ranges with 200-page safety pagination limit
Plugin marketplace defined in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json organized by category with schema validation
Plugins versioned independently with per-plugin author attribution
Plugin categories: development, productivity, learning, security
Dedupe uses two-phase agent architecture: parallel discovery then separate filtering agent
Issues with prior bot duplicate comment are skipped to prevent redundant comments
Locked issues and issues with assignees exempt from stale marking
All bot comments include Claude Code attribution footer
Issue sweep paginated with 10-page max per label, FIFO order (oldest first)
Commands inject live git context via backtick dynamic interpolation in frontmatter
Exactly one primary category label must always be applied to new issues; imperfect label preferred over none
